_readme: RULES-2 GO-finalisation assumption block ASM-1847..1856 (range 1847-1859 reserved; 1857-1859 deliberately unallocated for the coordinator's freeze-time corrections) — EMITTED by the build role (fable-build-4, owner writer-4, 2026-07-12) for central registration by the coordinator; registry/assumptions.jsonl is NOT touched by this build (same discipline as the 1440..1459 / 1800..1813 / 1814..1819 blocks).
Range 1847-1859 verified free against registry/assumptions.jsonl at emission time (registered neighbours: ...1846 [synthesis-v6] and 1860... [rules-1-d design]).

Scope: the GO finalisation of registry/experiments/rules-2.json under the maintainer's issue #24 decision (C) — B4/s3' strike, sequencing-gate re-registration, knull-analog sibling campaign (rules-2-knull) with its EXECUTED $0 leg, blocking-pilot pin, analysis successor, mock re-pin, GO cost bands (docs/next/design/rules-2-train-time.md Appendix C).

Tags: MEASURED = read off executed $0/CPU or pinned GPU-pilot artifacts in this tree; STIPULATED = design/protocol choices with rationale; EXTRAPOLATION carries resolution_path. No feasibility conclusion is stated in any row.

rows.claim: SEQUENCING GATE RE-REGISTRATION (GO): the PROPOSED-ASM-1420/1807 gate on RULES-2's GPU path is re-registered under the maintainer's issue #24 host-integration slot decision (C), 2026-07-12 — the rules-1-c mechanical readout landed INSTRUMENT-INVALID and decision (C) designates rules-2 (train-time internalisation, B4/s3' struck) as the slot's replacement instrument. Machine-readable form: the registry record's sequencing_gate block {reregistered: true, decision: 'C', authority citing issue #24}; enforcement: modal_rules2._launch_gates requires registry/verdicts/rules-1-c.json to EXIST and (verdict PASS or this block in the FROZEN record), plus FROZEN status, the staged-manifest match, the pinned green mock, the per-tier dry-plan, and --authorize-r2 for the R2 rung. No GPU spend on any branch without ALL gates.
rows.rationale: The pre-GO gate held every non-PASS branch pending the maintainer's slot decision; that decision now exists and must be carried machine-readably so the launch gate is enforceable code, not narrative.
rows.backing_ref: registry/experiments/rules-2.json sequencing_gate; poc/rules-2/modal/modal_rules2.py _launch_gates; docs/next/design/rules-2-train-time.md Appendix C

rows.claim: B4 + s3' + entailed2 STRUCK: the rules-1-c A3 verify-retry channel that B4 imported byte-identically is MEASURED VACUOUS at the 2-option entity operating point — (a) the landed rules-1-c campaign rows: attempts=1 on every A3 row, A3 correctness-identical to c1 on all item x seed cells; (b) the blocking pilot at the rules-2 operating point (IP-4, PROPOSED-ASM-1818): attempts_values=[1] on n=24 B4-pilot rows, b4_vacuous=true. B4 rows would be attempt-0 decoys; the verify-retry question is settled UN-INSTRUMENTABLE at 2-option scale (with functional-uniqueness dropped as gold-leaking, range/gender checks can never reject a well-formed 2-option answer). Consequences: arms B0/B1/B2/B3/B5/c1p; the s3' endpoint and the entailed2 common-2-option surface removed; Holm family {s1p, s2p, s4p}; the engine-at-inference price column becomes a DESCRIPTIVE cross-campaign pointer to the landed rules-1-c ledger (price rows from an INSTRUMENT-INVALID campaign are wall-clock measurements, never hypothesis evidence); NO break-even N*; B5 is the in-campaign efficiency comparator. Verify-retry repair, if ever, is a future rules-1-d record under the same issue.

KNULL ANALOG WIRING (the mandatory kernel-specific-attribution comparison; retires the MD-R2-8/PROPOSED-ASM-1438 deferral): sibling campaign registry/experiments/rules-2-knull.json, leg 1 = poc/rules-2/knull_analog.py ($0 CPU, deterministic): regenerate the rules-2 training examples twice over the IDENTICAL deterministic component set with the pinned twin engine — side A the kernel TBox (certificate.TBOX_PINNED; regeneration cross-checked byte-equal against the pinned data/rules2-train corpus, fail-closed ERR_KERNEL_REGEN), side B the knull plain-dictionary TBox (poc/rules-1-knull/inputs/tbox-knull, dir digest 15b79bb7... pin-verified against rules-1-knull-cert; same closed constraint grammar per PROPOSED-ASM-1402, shared ABox vocabulary URNs, fresh knull-profile URNs for TBox-introduced terms) — and compare per-id training surfaces (context, question, answer, options), proof sidecars reported separately. Leg 2 = the conditional GPU fine-tune (c5k at the rules-2 pinned HPs vs B2, matched, 3 seeds) activated ONLY by leg-1 divergence.
rows.rationale: The assessment mandates engine-derived vs plain-dictionary-derived TRAINING comparison for kernel-specific attribution; comparing the corpora byte-wise first is the $0 fail-closed way to decide whether the GPU contrast can be non-null at all.

rows.claim: KNULL ANALOG LEG-1 RESULT (EXECUTED 2026-07-12, $0 CPU): FULLY SURFACE-EQUIVALENT. Id sets identical (21,780 shared, 0 either-side-only); family-2 13,020/13,020 surface-equal (fraction 1.0; chain 5,720, cover 5,840, typing 1,460 all at 1.0); families 1+3 8,760/8,760; proof sidecars 0 divergent; knull-side builder aborts 0; skip/exclusion count ledgers identical across sides; kernel-side regeneration anchored byte-equal to the pinned corpus (kot-corpus-hash c46aaa4e...). Artifact poc/rules-2/results/knull-analog-result.json sha afcf09e882ae2fc3796eb72774eac4c46839169cc312fe0a717c8b4cfb1f89f1 (deterministic bytes: reproduction must be byte-exact).

KNULL GPU LEG PROHIBITION: with leg-1 family-2 surface_equal_fraction = 1.0, the c5k arm's training bytes ARE B2's training bytes and the c5k-vs-B2 contrast is 0 identically; launching the GPU leg is PROHIBITED (spend on a guaranteed-null contrast). Activation requires a record amendment carrying a FRESH leg-1 artifact showing divergence (e.g. after any change to either TBox or to the closed inventory) plus its own dry-plan.
rows.rationale: Fail-closed spend discipline: a registered-but-vacuous GPU leg run anyway would manufacture the appearance of an executed control while measuring nothing.

rows.claim: CLAIM-CAP RESOLUTION (ASM-1138 -> PROPOSED-ASM-1438 -> this row): the knull-closure comparison now EXISTS and EXECUTED, and its answer is DEFLATIONARY — on this closed kinship inventory the plain-dictionary rules source derives the byte-identical materialised training corpus, so the cap is PERMANENT in sharpened form: any rules-2 outcome (pass, fail, or inconclusive) supports at most 'engine-materialised entailments (derivable from EITHER rules source on this closed inventory) are internalisable by a small host', NEVER 'kernel-specific value'. This is two-sided: it neither validates the kernel nor privileges the dictionary; it says the closed constraint grammar, not its source, fixes the training content here.
rows.rationale: An executed control with an equivalence answer converts an open deferral into a permanent, citable scope limit; leaving the cap 'pending c5' after c5's analog executed would misstate the evidence state.

rows.claim: BLOCKING-PILOT PIN + ANALYSIS SUCCESSOR DISCIPLINE: (a) the PASSED blocking instrument pilot is pinned as pins.blocking_pilot (artifact poc/rules-2/results-incoming/20260712-165344-instrpilot/instrpilot-result.json sha 872477ef..., verdict PILOT-PASS-WITH-FLAGS, mode REAL, 2026-07-12) and is the satisfied PRE-FREEZE GATE (step 0) of the freeze protocol; pilot numbers are instrument-validity data only (ASM-1819). (b) Because that artifact's pin block carries analysis/rules_2.py sha 782bc9eb..., those bytes are NEVER edited: the GO analysis is the successor analysis/rules_2_go.py sha 58b4831f1b742ff934c28db2016c0fd284f2a0adfce1f4cf093d794c75639a54, pinned in pins.analysis_script with the predecessor note; delta = s3'/B4/entailed2 removal, Holm m=3, --rules1-primary-lb removed, engine price column demoted to descriptive; primary + s1'/s2'/s4' statistics carried verbatim (B=10000, PRNG seed 20260712, SESOI band, two-stage FT-seed x item BCa).
rows.rationale: Pinned bytes are immutable history (P2 1.1); a struck endpoint therefore requires a successor script pin, not an edit, and the pilot that proved the instrument must be referenced by the record it gates.

rows.claim: GO MOCK RE-PIN: validate_mock.py re-run GREEN at the GO surface (B4-less: monolithic --mock AND the 10 canonical shard jobs + merge_shards.py; 1,775 rows both paths; successor-analysis parity on every field except the disclosed process-measured metrics; verdict mapping resolves PASS on the planted stub gradient — mock mechanics only, never evidence; R1 + R2 dry-plans green). Artifact results/mock-validation.json sha 8d26424a..., tied to the GO staged-bytes manifest sha e8c7c78e189ab97a3922a6ea00e725f99c15dea5c6bbeeed43621509abad1a65 (modal_rules2 --print-manifest); the wrapper refuses full runs on any mismatch.

GO COST BANDS (supersede the PROPOSED-ASM-1454/1809 REWORK-3 bands; B4's eval-only job removed): R1 tier (14 independent parallel jobs) dry-plan est $4.30 / worst $6.46 (5.87 worst GPU-h; worst single job 0.57 h vs the 12 h Modal function timeout) vs usd_cap $18 / 14 GPU-h; R2 tier (7 jobs, coordinator-authorized second launch) worst $6.51 (worst single job 1.14 h); combined worst ~$12.97 vs the $35 outer ceiling. Bands are planning-constant extrapolations on actual entity-corpus chars, not measurements.
rows.backing_ref: poc/rules-2/rules2_runner.py --dry-plan output at GO build time; poc/rules-2/inputs/rules2-manifest.json planning
rows.resolution_path: the real campaign's results.training_ledger/eval_ledger walls and the Modal billing line resolve the bands; per PROPOSED-ASM-1429 the measured ledger replaces every extrapolated figure in the readout

rows.claim: GO FREEZE-READINESS + DRIFT DISCIPLINE: prereg-freeze --dry-run re-verified DRY-RUN-OK on the GO record at build time (non-fatal PAUSE on open EXTRAPOLATION rows is expected); the freeze itself, the central ASM registration (1420..1459, 1800..1819, 1847..1859) and the pre-freeze skeptic pass (flag list: ASM-1802/1446 refusal-surface bias, ASM-1804 s_mem anchor, ASM-1806 shortcut DESIGN-OPEN through the GPT-5.6 gate, ASM-1447 B1 token parity, ASM-1452 B3 deviation) remain the COORDINATOR'S steps. Any staged-byte change after the freeze-time pins.harness_manifest value requires a correction record (carries PROPOSED-ASM-1459 forward). This build performed NO freeze, NO GPU run, and states NO feasibility conclusion.
rows.rationale: Run-vs-design separation: the build role prepares a freezable record; freezing and spending are separated authorities.
